narration_text: Ever coughed up a tiny white rock from your throat and wondered, “What is that?”
visual_description: Close-up of a surprised person holding a tiny white pebble-like speck in their hand after coughing, with a simple throat-themed medical illustration in the background.
image_prompt: Vertical 9:16 YouTube Shorts frame, photorealistic close-up of a person looking confused and surprised after coughing, holding a tiny white pebble-sized object between fingers, subtle glow or highlight on the speck, clean medical-style background with faint illustration of a throat, bright natural lighting, sharp focus, realistic skin texture, modern social media health explainer style

narration_text: Those are tonsil stones. And they happen when bits of food, dead cells, mucus, and bacteria get trapped in the little cracks of your tonsils.
visual_description: Animated-style medical cutaway of a mouth and throat showing tonsils with small crevices trapping tiny particles like food bits, mucus, and bacteria.
image_prompt: Vertical 9:16 detailed medical illustration of the inside of a human mouth and throat, tonsils enlarged with visible crevices, tiny labeled-looking particles of food, dead cells, mucus, and bacteria trapped in the cracks, clean educational infographic aesthetic, bright colors, semi-realistic anatomy, high clarity, explanatory health content

narration_text: Over time, that trapped gunk hardens into a stone. It’s more common if you have deep tonsil crevices, post-nasal drip, dry mouth, poor oral hygiene, or frequent tonsillitis.
visual_description: Split visual showing trapped debris turning into a small white stone, alongside icons or subtle visuals for deep tonsil crevices, dry mouth, mucus drip, and oral hygiene.
image_prompt: Vertical 9:16 educational medical visualization, sequence-like composition showing debris in tonsil crevices transforming into a small hardened white tonsil stone, surrounding supportive icons or visuals for deep tonsil crevices, post-nasal drip, dry mouth, poor oral hygiene, and inflamed tonsils, clean infographic style, realistic anatomy, crisp labels-free design, bright clinical lighting

narration_text: They’re usually harmless, but they can cause bad breath, throat irritation, and that weird feeling like something is stuck back there.
visual_description: Person covering their nose and touching their throat, with subtle visual cues for bad breath and throat discomfort, plus a small sensation marker near the tonsils.
image_prompt: Vertical 9:16 realistic health explainer scene, person holding their throat and looking mildly uncomfortable, subtle visual cues for bad breath such as wavy odor lines, red irritated throat overlay, and a small sensation marker near the back of the mouth, clean bright background, modern medical content style, approachable and informative

narration_text: So if tonsil stones keep showing up, it’s not because your body is “dirty.” It’s because your tonsils are acting like tiny caves.
visual_description: Friendly end card style image of tonsils depicted as tiny cave-like openings with harmless stones inside, emphasizing that it’s a normal body issue rather than dirt.
